puzzles.headline: Nelson Mandela was released from prison after 27 years
Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walked on the Moon
The RMS Titanic sank in the North Atlantic
President John F. Kennedy was assassinated in Dallas
Tim Berners-Lee proposed the World Wide Web
A nuclear reactor exploded at Chernobyl in Ukraine
Allied forces landed at Normandy beaches in France
Apple released the first iPhone
The Watergate break-in occurred at the DNC headquarters
The United States dropped an atomic bomb on Hiroshima
The Soviet Union dissolved into independent states
The Wright Brothers flew the first powered airplane
Charles Lindbergh made the first solo transatlantic flight
The Cuban Missile Crisis brought the world to the brink of nuclear war
Rosa Parks refused to give up her bus seat in Montgomery
The Space Shuttle Challenger broke apart 73 seconds after launch
Terrorists attacked the World Trade Center and Pentagon
Japan attacked the US naval base at Pearl Harbor
Yuri Gagarin became the first human in space
French revolutionaries stormed the Bastille in Paris
The United States declared independence from Britain
MLK gave his I Have a Dream speech in Washington
Ray Tomlinson sent the first network email
Larry Page and Sergey Brin founded Google
Jeff Bezos founded Amazon as an online bookstore
Watson and Crick discovered the structure of DNA
Doctors identified a new disease called AIDS in New York
Christiaan Barnard performed the first heart transplant
Jonas Salk announced his polio vaccine worked
Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin
Kevin Systrom launched the photo-sharing app Instagram
Steve Chen and Chad Hurley launched YouTube
Jimmy Wales launched the free online encyclopedia Wikipedia
The Euro became the official currency of European Union members
Nintendo released the Game Boy handheld console
The stock market crashed, starting the Great Depression
Joseph Niepce took the first permanent photograph
Samuel Morse sent the first telegraph message
Johannes Gutenberg printed the first Bible with movable type
Confederate forces attacked Fort Sumter, starting the Civil War
A deadly flu pandemic began spreading worldwide
Elvis Presley appeared on television for the first time
Ray Kroc opened the first franchised McDonalds restaurant
Walt Disney opened Disneyland in California
Ruth Handler debuted the Barbie doll at the New York Toy Fair
Namco released the arcade game Pac-Man
Nintendo released Super Mario Bros for the NES
Alexey Pajitnov released the puzzle game Tetris
Sony launched the PlayStation video game console
Microsoft announced the Xbox gaming system
Steve Jobs introduced the iPod
Netflix began streaming movies over the internet
Daniel Ek launched the music streaming service Spotify
Mark Zuckerberg launched Facebook from his Harvard dorm
Jack Dorsey sent the first Twitter message
Evan Spiegel launched the disappearing photo app Snapchat
Satoshi Nakamoto published the Bitcoin white paper
Vitalik Buterin launched the Ethereum blockchain
Kevin McCoy minted the first NFT on the Namecoin blockchain
Christopher Columbus reached the Caribbean islands
King John signed the Magna Carta in England
William Shakespeare was born in Stratford-upon-Avon
George Lucas released Star Wars: A New Hope
Steven Spielberg released the thriller Jaws
Steven Spielberg released the film E.T.
Steven Spielberg released Jurassic Park in theaters
The Wachowskis released the film The Matrix
James Cameron released the highest-grossing film Avatar
Sony released the Walkman portable cassette player
The first VCR system used Sonys Umatic format
The first compact disc player was demonstrated in Japan
George Eastman founded the Kodak camera company
Edwin Land demonstrated the first Polaroid camera
Steven Sasson invented the first digital camera
Henry Ford began producing the Model T automobile
Tony Jannus flew the first commercial airline flight
The Soviet Union launched the first artificial satellite
NASA launched the Hubble Space Telescope into orbit
NASAs Sojourner rover landed on Mars
Johannes Gutenberg printed the first Bible with movable type
The last Roman emperor was deposed, ending the Western Roman Empire
A massive fire destroyed most of medieval London
The Beatles made their historic US debut on TV
400,000 people attended the Woodstock music festival
Bob Geldof organized Live Aid concerts in London and Philadelphia
MTV launched as the first 24-hour music video channel
Shawn Fning created the Napster file-sharing service
Steve Jobs opened the iTunes Store with 200,000 songs
Daniel Ek launched the music streaming service Spotify
Apple released the first iPhone
